article_title: Samsung Pursues Multi-Year Memory Deals With Google and Microsoft as Big Tech Races to Lock In AI Chip Supply

content_preview.title: Samsung Pursues Multi-Year Memory Deals With Google and Microsoft as Big Tech Races to Lock In AI Chip Supply
content_preview.summary: Samsung Electronics is negotiating three-to-five-year memory supply agreements with Google and Microsoft that could include more than 10 billion dollars in prepayments, as hyperscalers move to secure chip capacity amid a structural AI-driven shortage.
content_preview.body_excerpt: Samsung Electronics is in discussions with Google and Microsoft over long-term memory supply agreements spanning three to five years, a sharp departure from the quarterly and annual contracts that have traditionally defined the semiconductor memory market. The deals under negotiation could include [more than 10 billion dollars in upfront prepayments](https://wccftech.com/big-tech-is-paying-billions-to-lock-in-memory-supply/) from the two hyperscalers, according to industry reports, underscoring ...

editor_notes.content_quality: Excellent industry analysis with detailed contract mechanics, market data, and risk assessment. Strong structure moving from specific deals to market context to competitive landscape.
editor_notes.source_verification: WCCFTech (supply deals): Confirms $10B+ prepayments, 3-5 year terms, spot-linked pricing, Jun Young-hyun, Micron fully committed, SK Hynix 53%, custom chip programs. SiliconAngle: Confirms Q4 2025 20T won/$13.82B profit, 3x YoY, DRAM 313% price increase, Macquarie $311B projection. WCCFTech (AMD HBM4): Confirms Samsung HBM4, AMD MI455X/EPYC, NVIDIA Vera Rubin, 13 Gbps speeds.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: All financial figures, market share data, and technical specifications verified. Good balanced risk analysis.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral, analytical, and professional. No sensationalism.
editor_notes.originality: No prior coverage of Samsung multi-year memory deals. Unique topic.
editor_notes.overall_assessment: Strong semiconductor industry analysis with thorough sourcing. Ready for publication.
